title: trifolium polymorphum var. neuquenense
intro: Trifolium polymorphum var. neuquenense, also known as Neuquen clover, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the legume family (Fabaceae) native to South America. Traditionally, Neuquen clover has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections, providing benefits such as improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and boosted immune system. Neuquen clover can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
